expectations.text: Explicitly positions the customer as the hero and the brand/Isaac as the guide (not the hero)
expectations.evidence: Headline addresses customer directly: 'Your mission deserves to be heard in your own voice.' Subhead uses 'We help executive directors reclaim...' The SB7 Element Map explicitly states 'names the ED as hero; desire = being heard authentically.'

expectations.text: Names or applies the three levels of problem: external, internal, philosophical
expectations.evidence: SB7 Element Map explicitly names all three: External ('sector-generic messaging'), Internal ('you sound like everyone else / you've lost your own voice'), Philosophical ('mission-driven leaders shouldn't have to perform the sector to be taken seriously').

expectations.text: Positions the guide with both empathy and authority (competence)
expectations.evidence: SB7 Element Map states 'empathy (we see the grinding-down) + implicit authority (clear POV: Voice Liberation, named methodology).' The copy names Voice Liberation as a specific approach, signaling competence.

expectations.text: Surfaces both the external problem (generic messaging) and the internal problem (how it feels to sound like everyone else)
expectations.evidence: External: 'ground down into sector-speak' / 'your story sounds like everyone else's.' Internal: 'until your story sounds like everyone else's' captures the feeling of lost identity. Self-score confirms 'Names internal problem (sounding like everyone else, losing your own voice), not just external.'

expectations.text: Includes a clear direct call-to-action, not a vague 'learn more'
expectations.evidence: Direct CTA is 'Book a Discovery Call' -- specific and action-oriented. Transitional CTA also provided: 'See the Voice Liberation approach.'
